text.title: tool-use-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tool-use-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Passing large tool results (>50KB) back to the model without truncation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Very large tool results inflate context dramatically, pushing other messages out of the window and causing the model to lose track of earlier instructions. Tool result size: very large tool results (>50KB) sent back to the model inflate context dramatically; truncate or summarize large results before returning them.

failure-mode.description: Executing parallel tool calls (OpenAI default) when tools share mutable state.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parallel calls can produce race conditions — e.g., two concurrent writes to the same database row. Use sequential execution for stateful tools. Parallel tool calls (OpenAI default) can cause race conditions if tools share state; use tool_choice="auto" carefully with stateful tools.
